name: East Asian–Australasian Flyway
subtitle: East Asia to Australasia
speciesCount: ~490 migratory species including spoon-billed sandpiper, cranes, and shorebirds
description: The East Asian–Australasian Flyway is the most threatened of all global flyways, with the highest number of declining species. It stretches from Arctic Russia through East and Southeast Asia to Australia and New Zealand. The Yellow Sea tidal flats — shared by China and South Korea — are its most critical habitat, supporting millions of shorebirds, but over 65% have been lost to land reclamation since the 1950s. The Spoon-billed Sandpiper, one of the world's rarest birds (<500 individuals), depends entirely on this flyway.
